aids.title: CSND - VM Components
aids.content:
**C**PU (vCPU)
**S**torage (vDisk)
**N**etwork (vNIC)
**D**RAM (Memory)

Remember: 'Compute, Store, Network, Data - that's your VM!'
aids.forRemembering: Four main components of a virtual machine

aids.title: Hotel Room Analogy for Provisioning
aids.content:
**Thick Provisioning** = Booking a hotel room and paying for all nights upfront. The room is yours, guaranteed.

**Thin Provisioning** = Pay-as-you-stay hotel. You only pay for nights you actually use, but the hotel might overbook.

Thick = guaranteed but expensive upfront
Thin = efficient but risk of running out
aids.forRemembering: Difference between thick and thin provisioning

aids.title: Disk Format Memory
aids.content:
**V**MDK - VMware
**V**HDX - Hyper-V
**Q**COW2 - QEMU/KVM
**V**DI - VirtualBox
**R**AW - Universal

'VVQ VR' - Virtual disk formats
aids.forRemembering: Virtual disk formats and their platforms

aids.title: Live Migration Requirements
aids.content:
**SCNR** for Live Migration:
- **S**hared storage (SAN, NFS)
- **C**ompatible CPUs (same family)
- **N**etwork connectivity (management + vMotion)
- **R**esources available on destination

Missing any = migration fails!

aids.title: VM Lifecycle States
aids.content:
Created -> Powered Off <-> Running <-> Suspended
                 |
                 v
              Deleted

Key transitions:
- Start: Off -> Running
- Stop: Running -> Off
- Suspend: Running -> Suspended
- Resume: Suspended -> Running

aids.title: Snapshot Best Practices
aids.content:
Snapshots are NOT backups!

**DO:**
- Use for short-term (testing, updates)
- Delete after use
- Limit chain to 2-3 levels

**DON'T:**
- Keep for weeks/months
- Use as backup strategy
- Create deep chains (performance hit)
